# magicpath-ai CLI Reference

<!-- Generated by `magicpath-ai docs --format md`. Do not edit by hand. -->

MagicPath CLI — agent-first interface to MagicPath. Search, inspect, and install UI components ("designs") into apps; create and edit designs on the MagicPath canvas from code; manage projects, themes (design systems), revisions, images, and teams. Every command emits a JSON envelope: {ok: true, data, meta} on stdout, {ok: false, error: {code, message, suggestion}} on stderr. Start with `context` to see auth, teams, open projects, and the current canvas selection.

All commands emit a JSON envelope: `{ok: true, data, meta}` on stdout (exit 0) or
`{ok: false, error: {code, message, suggestion?}}` on stderr (exit 1). Paginated
commands accept `--limit <n>` and `--cursor <token>` and return `meta.page.nextCursor`.

### `context`

One-call context for agents: who is authenticated, their teams, the project(s) open on the MagicPath canvas, and any selected components or images.

- `--no-selection` — Skip reading the live canvas selection
- `--timeout <ms>` — Canvas connect timeout in milliseconds

## `auth`

Authentication: log in, log out, and check status

### `auth login`

Log in to your MagicPath account

- `--code <code>` — Exchange an authorization code non-interactively

### `auth logout`

Log out of your MagicPath account

### `auth status`

Show local auth status without a network call

## `project`

Projects: list, create, rename, duplicate, move, share, and inspect canvases

### `project list`

List projects you can access (personal and team)

- `--team <nameOrId>` — Only projects in this team
- `--personal` — Only personal projects
- `--limit <n>` — Maximum results per page (1-100)
- `--cursor <token>` — Opaque cursor from meta.page.nextCursor

### `project get <id>`

Get a project by id

### `project create`

Create a project (personal or in a team)

- `--name <name>` — Project name (defaults to an auto-generated name)
- `--team <nameOrId>` — Create the project in this team

### `project rename <id>`

Rename a project

- `--name <name>` — New project name

### `project delete <id>`

Delete a project

### `project duplicate <id>`

Duplicate a project, including its components

### `project move <id>`

Move a project into a team workspace

- `--team <nameOrId>` — Destination team

### `project canvas <id>`

Snapshot of a project canvas: components and images with positions

### `project share-link <id>`

Get (or create) a public share link for a project

- `--revoke` — Revoke all share links and disable sharing

## `component`

Components: list, get, rename, delete, duplicate, copy, revisions, screenshots

### `component list <projectId>`

List components in a project

- `--name <substr>` — Filter by name (case-insensitive substring)
- `--created-by <userId>` — Filter to components created or edited by this user ID
- `--sort-by <field>` — Sort by field (name, createdAt)
- `--order <dir>` — Sort direction (asc, desc)
- `--status <status>` — Revision status filter (COMPLETED, PENDING, FAILED)
- `--limit <n>` — Maximum results per page (1-100)
- `--cursor <token>` — Opaque cursor from meta.page.nextCursor

### `component get <idOrGeneratedName>`

Get a component by ID or generatedName

### `component rename <id>`

Rename a component

- `--name <name>` — New component name

### `component delete <id>`

Delete one or more components

### `component duplicate <id>`

Duplicate a component within its project

### `component copy-to <id>`

Copy a component to another project

- `--project <targetProjectId>` — Target project ID
- `--revision <revisionId>` — Revision to copy (defaults to the selected revision)

### `component revisions <id>`

List a component's revision history (newest first)

### `component select-revision <id>`

Set a component's selected revision (reverts the canvas to that revision)

- `--revision <revisionId>` — Revision ID to select

### `component screenshot <id>`

Render a PNG screenshot of a component revision

- `--revision <revisionId>` — Revision to render (defaults to the selected revision)
- `--width <px>` — Viewport width in pixels (100-4000)
- `--height <px>` — Viewport height in pixels (100-4000)
- `--scale <n>` — Device scale factor (1-4)
- `--out <path>` — Output file path (default: magicpath-screenshot-<id>.png)

## `theme`

Themes (design systems): list, get, create, update, delete

### `theme list`

List themes (design systems)

- `--team <nameOrId>` — List themes for a specific team

### `theme get <idOrName>`

Get a theme definition (CSS variables, fonts, styling prompt)

- `--team <nameOrId>` — Look up theme within a specific team

### `theme create`

Create a theme (design system)

- `--name <name>` — Theme name
- `--file <path>` — Path to a JSON file with the theme data
- `--json <inline>` — Inline JSON string with the theme data

### `theme update <id>`

Update a theme name and/or data

- `--name <name>` — New theme name
- `--file <path>` — Path to a JSON file with the new theme data
- `--json <inline>` — Inline JSON string with the new theme data

### `theme delete <id>`

Delete a theme

## `team`

Teams (read-only): list teams and their members

### `team list`

List teams the authenticated user belongs to

### `team members <team>`

List members of a team

## `image`

Project canvas images: add, list, delete, duplicate, and resolve URLs

### `image add <projectId> <pathOrUrl>`

Add an image to a project canvas from a local file path or a URL

- `--name <name>` — Display name for the image (defaults to the file name)
- `--x <x>` — Canvas x position (default 0)
- `--y <y>` — Canvas y position (default 0)
- `--width <width>` — Width in canvas units (defaults to the intrinsic width)
- `--height <height>` — Height in canvas units (defaults to the intrinsic height)

### `image list <projectId>`

List the images on a project canvas

### `image delete <id>`

Delete one or more images from a project canvas

- `--project <projectId>` — The project the images belong to

### `image duplicate <id>`

Duplicate an image on its project canvas

### `image url <id>`

Get a fresh, viewable URL for an image

### `search <query>`

Server-side search over component name and generatedName in every project the user can access. Filter with --team, --personal, or --project.

- `--team <nameOrId>` — Search only within a specific team
- `--personal` — Search only personal projects
- `--project <projectId>` — Search only within a specific project
- `--limit <n>` — Maximum results per page (1-100)
- `--cursor <token>` — Opaque cursor from meta.page.nextCursor

## `code`

Create and edit canvas components from local code

### `code start`

Start a pending create or edit session before writing code

- `--project <projectId>` — Target MagicPath project ID for create
- `--component <componentId>` — Existing MagicPath component ID for edit
- `--revision <revisionId>` — Start editing this revision. Defaults to the component's currently selected revision.
- `--dir <dir>` — Working directory to initialize
- `--name <name>` — Component name
- `--width <px>` — Canvas component width for create
- `--height <px>` — Canvas component height for create

### `code read <componentId>`

Fetches the editable source files of a component into a working directory without starting an edit session. Run `code start --component <componentId>` before submitting edits.

- `--dir <dir>` — Working directory to write
- `--revision <revisionId>` — Pull this revision of the component. Defaults to the component's currently selected revision.

### `code submit`

Submit changed editable files for the component in this working directory

- `--dir <dir>` — Working directory containing magicpath-code.json
- `--no-wait` — Return immediately instead of waiting for the build job to finish
- `--interval <ms>` — Polling interval while waiting
- `--width <px>` — Updated canvas component width
- `--height <px>` — Updated canvas component height

### `code status <jobId>`

Fetch external-agent build job status

### `install <generatedNames>`

Fetches one or more components from the MagicPath registry, writes their files to src/components/magicpath, and installs missing dependencies.

- `--dir <dir>` — Custom path for components (default: src/components/magicpath)
- `--force` — Overwrite existing files
- `--dry-run` — Show what would happen without writing files

### `inspect <generatedName>`

Inspect a MagicPath component's source code without installing

### `installed`

List MagicPath components installed in the current project

- `--dir <dir>` — Custom components path (default: src/components/magicpath)

### `open <target>`

Resolves a project id or component generatedName to its MagicPath URL. Opens the OS browser only for an interactive human or with --browser; agents receive the URL in the result instead.

- `--browser` — Force-open the OS browser
